stem: A graph shows the estimated melting point y, in degrees Celsius, of a normal alkane with a molecular weight of x grams per mole, where 1 ≤ x ≤ 320. Which statement is the best interpretation of the point (210, -45) on the graph?

answerOptions.content: A normal alkane with a molecular weight of 210 grams per mole has an estimated melting point of -45 degrees Celsius.
A normal alkane with a molecular weight of -45 grams per mole has an estimated melting point of 210 degrees Celsius.
The minimum estimated melting point for normal alkanes corresponds to an alkane with a molecular weight of 210 grams per mole and an estimated melting point of -45 degrees Celsius.
The maximum estimated melting point for normal alkanes corresponds to an alkane with a molecular weight of 210 grams per mole and an estimated melting point of -45 degrees Celsius.

rationale: Choice A is correct. The variable x is the molecular weight in grams per mole, and y is the estimated melting point in degrees Celsius. So for the point (210, -45), 210 represents the molecular weight and -45 represents the corresponding estimated melting point. Choice B swaps the meanings of x and y. Choices C and D incorrectly describe the point as a minimum or maximum.
